WEEKLY REPORT: @CliffCircuit + @timharris707
Period: Mar 8–15, 2026
Generated: Sun Mar 15, 2026 9:00 AM PT

Overview

This was a high-output week, especially on @CliffCircuit. The two accounts are clearly reinforcing each other: Cliff is publishing repeated proof-of-pattern content around service-business follow-up gaps, while Tim is translating that into founder/operator commentary and “building in public with AI” framing.

The good news:
- Posting cadence is strong
- Messaging is getting tighter and more consistent
- Cross-account support is working
- GitHub activity spiked hard today, which gives Tim real product-building proof to talk about

The bad news:
- Audience growth is still small
- Engagement is modest
- No real viral breakout yet
- Most mentions on @CliffCircuit are still coming from Tim, not outside accounts

Follower stats

Current follower counts:
- @CliffCircuit: 5 followers
- @timharris707: 8 followers

Growth note:
- I do not have a clean Mar 8 follower snapshot stored, so exact 7-day follower delta is not reliable from available data.
- Longer-view context: earlier memory snapshots showed roughly 6 followers for @CliffCircuit and 4 for @timharris707 around late Feb / Mar 1, so Tim appears up overall since then, while Cliff looks flat to slightly down depending on snapshot timing.

Posting volume

@CliffCircuit
- Very active this week
- Roughly 17+ posts visible in the 7-day window from the retrieved set
- Main theme: service-business revenue loss caused by poor follow-up timing
- Repeatedly mapped this pattern across industries:
  - roofing
  - real estate
  - HVAC
  - plumbing
  - pest control
  - house cleaning
  - physical therapy
  - painting
  - auto detailing
  - flooring
  - catering
  - plus reflective/meta posts on stripping systems down to essentials

@timharris707
- Roughly 11 posts visible in the 7-day window
- Main themes:
  - interpreting Cliff’s industry findings
  - practical AI use vs flashy demos
  - consistency > capability
  -

Morning briefing for Monday, March 16

Overnight X activity

@CliffCircuit
- Follower count: 5
- Overnight engagement was quiet.
- Recent posts visible in the overnight window were a run of low-engagement publishes, including:
  - HVAC service agreements expire in silence
  - Pest control missed-call angle
  - Two electrician-related posts
  - Chiropractor dropout angle
  - Dog groomer rebooking angle
- None of the recent Cliff posts show replies yet. Views are still very low and mostly in the 0–5 range.

@timharris707
- Follower count: 8
- One fresh post visible overnight:
  - PT dropout reaction to @CliffCircuit
- No replies or meaningful engagement on the latest Tim post so far.
- No overnight follower change detected.

Replies / mentions needing response
- No overnight replies to either account were found.
- No overnight direct replies to @timharris707 were found.
- One fresh mention of @CliffCircuit came from Tim’s own PT-dropout post. That does not need a response.

Scheduled posts for today
From the current content schedule, today’s queue is loaded and pending:
- 8:00 AM PT — HVAC service agreements expire in silence
- 9:08 AM PT — Tim post reacting to Cliff’s PT dropout piece
- 10:16 AM PT — Cliff standalone post: “Most businesses think AI is for answering questions...”
- 11:24 AM PT — Daycares lose enrollments in 48 hours
- 12:32 PM PT — Tim post: “The thing about AI that took me the longest to absorb...”
- 1:40 PM PT — Cliff standalone post about reviews / follow-up
- 2:48 PM PT — Insurance clients don’t switch agencies
- 3:56 PM PT — Tim gym-members-drift post
- Then more evening slots continue through the rest of the day.

Issues / flags
- The schedule is populated, but all slots still showed as pending at briefing time, including the 8:00 AM slot. Worth confirming the first publish actually fires on time.
- There was a scheduler/path error last night involving:
  - /Users/openclaw/workspace/samantha/publish-due-items.json.tmp
  That looked like a missin

# Project: Portal Token Usage — Cost Chart + Session Details

**Repo:** `CliffCircuit/cliffcircuit-ai`

## ⚠️ CRITICAL: File Split (2026-03-10)
tokens.js was split into 7 modules. **NEVER read a full file.** Use jCodeMunch.

## Files (load order in tokens.html)
1. `portal/tokens-core.js` (~490 lines) — state (window._global*), multi-select widget, helpers (_isAll, _inFilter, _fmtK, _fmtDur, _fmtTs), filter controls
2. `portal/tokens-detail.js` (~417 lines) — session detail expand/collapse panel, gateway WebSocket, model pricing, task tooltips
3. `portal/tokens-data.js` (~507 lines) — Supabase queries (loadAndRenderAgentSessions), active sessions, ticket helpers, stuck session detection
4. `portal/tokens-chart.js` (~467 lines) — stacked cost bar chart (by agent/model/task), legend toggles, time buckets
5. `portal/tokens-table.js` (~304 lines) — grouped agent table (renderGrouped), raw session table (renderRawTable)
6. `portal/tokens-cards.js` (~229 lines) — agent portrait cards (renderSummaryPortraits), stat summary cards (_updateSummaryCards), renderTokenSections orchestrator
7. `portal/tokens-init.js` (~122 lines) — refreshAllData, initPortal, auto-refresh (30s), Supabase realtime, tooltip IIFE

Also: `portal/portal-common.js` (~490 lines) — shared helpers, nav, initPortal, Supabase client
Also: `portal/tokens.html` (~335 lines) — HTML/CSS markup

## Cross-File Shared State
All shared vars use `window.*` (e.g., window._isAll, window._globalRange).
Function declarations (function keyword) are globally accessible across files.

- `_initMultiSelect(containerId, opts)` — reusable checkb

**What was done (TKT-0045):**
1. **Select All / Unselect All toggle** added to the top of every multi-select filter dropdown (Agent, Task, Provider, Model) via the shared `_initMultiSelect` widget
2. **Toggle logic:** Shows "Unselect All" when all options are checked → clicking unchecks all (uses `__none__` sentinel so `_inFilter` matches nothing). Shows "Select All" when any option is unchecked → clicking clears the filter array to show everything
3. **Styling:** Toggle is centered, indigo-colored (#818cf8), font-weight 500, 11px — visually distinct from regular options. Separator line (`.ms-option-sep`) below the toggle
4. **Edge cases handled:** Individual item clicks from "none" state correctly start fresh selection; "Clear filters" works from none state; grouped option display preserves the toggle
5. **Bonus: Grouped task dropdown** — Task filter now groups items by owning agent (with uppercase headers) when multiple agents have tasks, falls back to flat list for single agent

NO_REPLY
